articles.headline: France pass toughest World Cup test yet in beating feisty Paraguay
articles.description: France haven't really been challenged yet at this World Cup in the way Paraguay tested them on Sunday, but Mbappe & Co. were able to stay cool in the sweltering heat and advance to the quarterfinals.

articles.headline: The master of the half-time team talk: Explaining why Thomas Tuchel at England works
articles.description: ESPN's Tom Hamilton spoke to figures from Thomas Tuchel's past who witnessed his rise to become England's ideal boss.

images.name: Weber [1296x729]
images.caption: Benjamin Weber (right) worked with Thomas Tuchel at Mainz, Borussia Dortmund, Paris Saint-Germain and Chelsea.

images.name: Ströll  [1296x729]

articles.headline: Mbappé, France cruise past Paraguay 1-0 into the WC QFs
articles.description: Kylian Mbappé fires home the lone goal of the game from the penalty spot as France fight their way past Paraguay and into the World Cup quarterfinals.

images.name: Mbappé, France cruise past Paraguay 1-0 into the WC QFs

article.headline: Kylian Mbappé guides France to World Cup quarterfinals, Paraguay eliminated
article.description: Kylian Mbappé scored his 19th career World Cup goal, and France survived stifling heat to beat Paraguay 1-0 on Saturday, sending Les Bleus into the quarterfinals for the fourth straight time.

article.story:
Kylian Mbappé scored his 19th career World Cup goal, and France survived stifling heat to beat Paraguay 1-0 on Saturday, sending Les Bleus into the quarterfinals for the fourth straight time.

France advanced to play Morocco on Thursday in Foxborough, Massachusetts.

With an extreme heat warning in effect throughout the match as temperatures hovered around 38C, Mbappé finally broke through against a physical, defensive-minded Paraguay side when he converted a penalty kick in the 70th minute.

That was enough for Les Bleus, whose red, white and blue-clad fans looked plenty patriotic on July Fourth in Philadelphia, where the United States was founded exactly 250 years earlier.

Mbappé was awarded the penalty when Diego Gómez was called for tripping after a video review.

Then he stutter-stepped on his way to his 19th goal in 19 World Cup appearances, one behind career record holder Lionel Messi of Argentina. Mbappé and Messi each have seven goals in this tournament to top the Golden Boot race.

Mbappé won that award four years ago in Qatar, but Messi and Argentina beat France in the final.

Mbappé botched a breakaway attempt in the second half, and Manu Koné had his top-corner shot knocked away by goalkeeper Orlando Gill after he was wiped out in the netting by Ousmane Dembélé only moments earlier.

Gill also stopped two strong attempts by Mbappé in second-half stoppage time.
